samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module. Detects a pulse when data_in transitions from 0 to 1 to 0 (3 cycles). Outputs data_out as 1 during the last cycle of the pulse and returns to 0 otherwise.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="S1">First state after detecting rising edge.</state>
        <state id="S2">Second state after detecting falling edge.</state>
        <state id="S3">Final state after completing the pulse detection.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="state" width="2">State register to track the current state of pulse detection.</register>
        <wire id="next_state" width="2">Next state based on current state and inputs.</wire>
        <always sensitivity="posedge clk or negedge rst_n">
            <if condition="!rst_n">
                <assign state=S0; data_out=0;>
                    <comment>Reset state and output to initial values.</comment>
                </assign>
            </if>
            <else>
                <assign state=next_state;>
                    <comment>Update state to next state on clock edge.</comment>
                </assign>
            </else>
        </always>
        <always sensitivity="*">
            <case expression="state">
                <when value="S0">
                    <if condition="data_in==1'b1">
                        <assign next_state=S1; data_out=0;>
                            <comment>Transition to S1 on rising edge of data_in.</comment>
                        </assign>
                    </if>
                    <else>
                        <assign next_state=S0; data_out=0;>
                            <comment>Stay in S0 if data_in is 0.</comment>
                        </assign>
                    </else>
                </when>
                <when value="S1">
                    <if condition="data_in==1'b0">
                        <assign next_state=S2; data_out=0;>
                            <comment>Transition to S2 on falling edge of data_in.</comment>
                        </assign>
                    </if>
                    <else>
                        <assign next_state=S1; data_out=0;>
                            <comment>Stay in S1 if data_in is still 1.</comment>
                        </assign>
                    </else>
                </when>
                <when value="S2">
                    <if condition="data_in==1'b1">
                        <assign next_state=S3; data_out=0;>
                            <comment>Transition to S3 on rising edge of data_in.</comment>
                        </assign>
                    </if>
                    <else>
                        <assign next_state=S0; data_out=0;>
                            <comment>Return to S0 if data_in is 0.</comment>
                        </assign>
                    </else>
                </when>
                <when value="S3">
                    <assign next_state=S0; data_out=1;>
                        <comment>Set data_out to 1 and return to S0 after completing the pulse detection.</comment>
                    </assign>
                </when>
            </case>
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic implementing pulse detection based on the state machine described above.</implementation>
</module>
